title: Ochna axillaris
taxonomy: Ochna axillaris belongs to the family Ochnaceae, commonly known as the chafftree family. This plant is a species of flowering plant that is native to South Africa. It is classified within the genus Ochna, which includes approximately 40 species of flowering plants. The genus Ochna is further classified within the order Malpighiales. Ochna axillaris is a small shrub with distinctive flowers that are often used as a food source for various insects.

preparations: Ochna axillaris, also known as the small mouse bush or small ironwood, is a plant native to South Africa. intro: Ochna axillaris, also known as the small sourberry or Ochna, is a small shrub or tree native to Australia, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its health benefits, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ochna axillaris include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antiproliferative, and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Ochna axillaris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
